No Poverty.reason: The 'Zopa: Peer-to-Peer Lending' case offers a tenuous link to microfinance (Target 1.4), which can aid poverty reduction. General social responsibility is too broad.

Zero Hunger.reason: The '鮮乳坊' case, a dairy company, is discussed for business strategy. This offers a very tenuous, indirect link to food production systems, but not directly to hunger.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: There is no explicit or inferable evidence in the course syllabus related to health, well-being, or healthcare.

Quality Education.reason: The course *is* tertiary education, explicitly aiming to provide advanced professional knowledge and develop analytical, decision-making, and entrepreneurial skills for employment (Targets 4.3, 4.4).
evidence: 習得相關進階專業知識
創業態度與面對變革的能力

Gender Equality.reason: There is no explicit or inferable evidence in the course syllabus related to gender equality. 'Social responsibility' is too broad to imply this SDG.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: There is no explicit or inferable evidence in the course syllabus related to clean water and sanitation.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: There is no explicit or inferable evidence in the course syllabus related to affordable and clean energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course directly covers 'Growth Strategy,' 'Innovation,' 'Startup Strategy,' and 'long-run performance of a corporation,' explicitly supporting economic growth, productivity, and entrepreneurship (Targets 8.1, 8.2, 8.3).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course explicitly covers 'Innovation,' 'Startup Strategy,' 'Platform Strategy,' and 'Corporate Strategy,' directly promoting industrialization, innovation, and technological upgrading (Targets 9.2, 9.3, 9.5).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The 'Zopa: Peer-to-Peer Lending' case offers a weak, indirect link to financial inclusion, which can contribute to reducing inequalities (Target 10.5).

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: There is no explicit or inferable evidence in the course syllabus related to sustainable cities and communities.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: Core Competency B explicitly requires students to 'identify sustainability considerations of business situations,' directly aligning with encouraging sustainable business practices (Target 12.6).

Climate Action.reason: 'Environmental scanning' and 'sustainability considerations' (Core Competency B) offer a very tenuous, indirect link to climate action, as climate is part of the broader environment.

Life Below Water.reason: There is no explicit or inferable evidence in the course syllabus related to life below water.

Life on Land.reason: There is no explicit or inferable evidence in the course syllabus related to life on land.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: Core Competency B's focus on 'ethics' and 'social responsibility' offers a weak, indirect link to corporate accountability and transparent institutions (Targets 16.5, 16.6).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course explicitly covers 'International Strategy,' 'Globalization,' and 'Strategic Alliance,' which are direct forms of multi-stakeholder partnerships and international cooperation (Targets 17.16, 17.17).
